body.title: CLOSED: Attractor-first graph traversal in cortex.search
body.result: attractor-first BFS in cortex.search Phase 0; depth=2/limit=20 from TWM anchors; CP/ID fallback at depth=2/limit=50; 5 tests; commit 3bc2fdb6
body.description:
cortex.search currently traverses from all 20 CP/ID roots at depth=3, touching up to 200 nodes and issuing 100+ parameter IN queries (282-387ms each). This is expensive and retrieves everything rather than what's relevant to the current focus. The biological model: attention is already focused (attractor set by user's message), so traversal should START from the attractor and follow its edges — not from all identity roots. Supersedes D199 (CP-roots traversal) for the hot path.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py (traverse_from call at ~line 2508)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** In scope: change traversal seed from all CP/ID roots to current TWM attractor node(s); depth=2 from attractor; limit=20. Fall back to CP/ID roots at depth=2, limit=50 when no attractor exists (first message of session) or attractor has no graph links. Out of scope: changing the attractor weight mechanism (G50); changing the factual-kw-supplement path.
**Test plan:** Set a known attractor memory with specific child links. Run cortex.search. Assert returned candidates include the attractor's children and NOT arbitrary CP-root descendants. Measure IN query param count before/after — should drop from 100+ to <25.
**Pass condition:** Slow query log shows no IN queries with >30 parameters during a normal Igor turn. Narrative turn log shows retrieved memories are linked to the current conversation topic, not generic CP/ID tree nodes.
